title: America’s Greatest Hot Dog
description: "The price and quality of the $1.50 Costco frank haven’t budged in 40 years. It’s the last—and best—deal in the country."
hook: Costco's $1.50 hot dog hasn't changed in 40 years—a deliberate act of defiance in an economy designed to make everything worse.

title: Hélène Bessette and the Novel as Arc Lamp
description: “Though a novel, the book can also be read as a cacophonous speech-collage-turned-philosophical-investigation of crime and punishment.”
hook: A novelist writes like she's making a speech collage about crime and punishment—form and philosophy become indistinguishable.

title: 'You Won't Get Free of It' explores the complicated mother-daughter bond
description: The New Yorker writer Rachel Aviv spent years reporting stories about mothers and daughters searching for each other. When she became a mom, she saw everything she wrote differently.
hook: A New Yorker writer spent years reporting on mothers and daughters, then motherhood made her reread all her own sentences differently.

title: Rosebush Pruning review – a dark-hearted, button-pushing hoot
description: Karim Aïnouz turns Efthimis Filippou's twisted screenplay into a gloriously deranged portrait of rich-kid rot.
hook: A filmmaker turns a twisted screenplay into pure derangement—family dysfunction so grotesque it becomes funny, then stops being funny.

title: Skill nostalgia
description:
Is all the beekeeping, baking and leatherwork just escapist fantasy or the start of a radically human approach to work?

- by Joshua Habgood-Coote
hook: Everyone's learning beekeeping and woodworking again. But what are we actually nostalgic for—the skill or the fantasy of slowing down?
